Card

Stable

Flexible card container for grouping related content with multiple variants, padding options, and composable sub-components.

Overview

The Card component provides a composable content container with four visual variants and four padding sizes. It ships with CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent, and CardFooter sub-components for consistent layout patterns. The Card is the foundation for dashboards, settings panels, and content sections.

Live Demo

Click the variant buttons to see the Card component change styles live.

Account Settings

Active

Manage your account preferences, notification settings, and security options from this panel.

Click the variant buttons above to see the card style change live.

Variants

Four variants give you flexibility from subtle backgrounds to elevated surfaces.

Default

White background with subtle shadow and light border.

Elevated

Deeper shadow for emphasis — great for modals or dropdowns.

Bordered

Heavy 2px border for clear visual separation.

Flat

No shadow or border — subtle background tint.

card-variants.tsx 
import { Card } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function CardVariants() {
  return (
    <div className="grid gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2">
      <Card variant="default">
        <p>Default card content</p>
      </Card>
      <Card variant="elevated">
        <p>Elevated card content</p>
      </Card>
      <Card variant="bordered">
        <p>Bordered card content</p>
      </Card>
      <Card variant="flat">
        <p>Flat card content</p>
      </Card>
    </div>
  );
}

Composition Pattern

The recommended way to build card UIs is using the composable sub-components.

Account Settings

Manage your account preferences and notification settings from one place.

card-composition.tsx 
import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent, CardFooter } from '@storyhouse/components';
import { Button } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function CardComposition() {
  return (
    <Card variant="default" padding="md">
      <CardHeader>
        <CardTitle>Account Settings</CardTitle>
        <Button variant="ghost" size="sm">Edit</Button>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        Manage your account preferences and notification settings.
      </CardContent>
      <CardFooter>
        <Button variant="secondary" size="sm">Cancel</Button>
        <Button variant="primary" size="sm">Save</Button>
      </CardFooter>
    </Card>
  );
}

Padding Options

Four padding levels — none, sm, md, and lg — give you precise control over spacing.

card-padding.tsx 
import { Card } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function CardPadding() {
  return (
    <>
      <Card padding="none">No padding — for image-heavy cards</Card>
      <Card padding="sm">Compact spacing</Card>
      <Card padding="md">Default spacing (1.5rem)</Card>
      <Card padding="lg">Spacious layout (2rem)</Card>
    </>
  );
}

Common Use Cases

Profile

Active

Settings panels with header, status badge, and action footer.

Revenue

$12.4k

Dashboard widgets with metric display and minimal chrome.

Edge Cases & Error States

Nested Cards

Avoid nesting Cards directly — a Card inside another Card will lose background visibility when using the same variant. If nesting is necessary, use a different variant for the inner card:

card-nesting.tsx 
import { Card } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function CardNesting() {
  return (
    <Card variant="default">
      <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold">Outer Card</h3>
      {/* Use flat variant inside another card */}
      <Card variant="flat" padding="sm" className="mt-3">
        <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">Nested flat card works cleanly inside default.</p>
      </Card>
    </Card>
  );
}

Empty Card (No Children)

A Card with no children renders as an empty container with only padding and background. This is valid but provides no visual benefit. Always provide meaningful content.

Overflow Content

Cards don't clip overflow by default. Long content will naturally extend the card height. If you need a fixed-height card, set max-h-* via className and wrap content in a scrollable container inside CardContent.

card-scrollable.tsx 
import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function CardScrollable() {
  return (
    <Card className="max-h-80">
      <CardHeader>
        <CardTitle>Activity Log</CardTitle>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        <div className="max-h-48 space-y-2 overflow-y-auto">
          {/* Long list of items */}
        </div>
      </CardContent>
    </Card>
  );
}

Best Practices & Usage Guidelines

Heading Hierarchy

Always use the as prop on CardTitle to match the correct heading level for your page structure. If a Card appears under an h2 section, the CardTitle should use as="h3" (which is the default).

Layout Patterns

  • Use card grids with grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 for dashboard layouts
  • Keep card content focused — one concept per card
  • Use CardHeader for titles and actions, CardContent for body, CardFooter for CTAs
  • Add hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow via className for interactive cards
  • Wrap clickable cards in an <a> tag or use role="button"

Accessibility

  • Cards are semantic <div> elements — use correct heading hierarchy
  • Interactive cards need explicit roles: role="button" tabIndex=0 or wrap in an anchor
  • Keyboard event handlers (onKeyDown for Enter/Space) are required on interactive cards
  • CardFooter uses flex layout with gap-2 for proper button spacing

Implementation Details

Composable Sub-Component Pattern

Card uses a composable architecture with four sub-components: CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent, and CardFooter. Each is independently exported and can be used outside a Card parent if needed, though this is uncommon.

Padding Propagation

Padding is applied to the outer Card wrapper, not to individual sub-components. This means CardHeader and CardContent naturally inherit the card's padding without needing their own padding classes. The padding="none" variant removes all padding for image-heavy cards or custom layouts.

Dark Mode

  • All variant backgrounds have dark: counterparts (dark:bg-gray-900, dark:bg-gray-800)
  • Borders use dark:border-gray-800 for default/elevated and dark:border-gray-700 for bordered
  • Text colors use dark:text-gray-300 / dark:text-gray-400 for readability

Props

Card

Prop Type Default Description
variant "default" | "elevated" | "bordered" | "flat" "default" Visual style variant.
padding "none" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" "md" Internal padding amount.
className string - Additional CSS classes merged via tailwind-merge.
children React.ReactNode - Content rendered inside the card.

CardHeader

Prop Type Default Description
children React.ReactNode - Header content. Typically a title and an action.
className string - Additional CSS classes.

CardTitle

Prop Type Default Description
as "h1" | "h2" | "h3" | "h4" | "h5" | "h6" "h3" HTML heading tag to render.
children React.ReactNode - Title text or content.
className string - Additional CSS classes.

CardContent

Prop Type Default Description
children React.ReactNode - Content rendered in the body area.
className string - Additional CSS classes.

CardFooter

Prop Type Default Description
children React.ReactNode - Footer content. Typically buttons or actions.
className string - Additional CSS classes.

Accessibility

  • Card is a semantic <div> — use heading hierarchy correctly with CardTitle as="h2"
  • Interactive cards should use role="button" or wrap in an anchor
  • CardFooter uses flex gap-2 to properly space action buttons
  • Use CardHeader with proper heading levels for screen reader navigation
